For the sake of simplicity, we said that ar mor was only of one type. Actually, of course, ‘many kinds of armor do provide some protec tion against more than one type of weapon. saa trivial example, you could buff a suit of plate mail toa fine finish, so it would provide some protection against lasers as well as im- pact weapons. ‘Multiple-type armor just has armor ratings including more than one letter-number com- bination — for instance, that polished plate ‘might be 13/11. Laminated composite battle ar- ‘mor might be L4/13/P2/AP6, We leave this up to you

Drawing, Ammunition, and Reloading Drawing weapons in desperation when am- bushed and hunching dawn behind a rack to reload are valuable dramatic tools in any 
actionadventure game. On the other hand they involve a cena amount of bookkeeping 
I Drawing Weapons {fa citizen does not have a weapon in hand, he cannot use a weapon until he draws one if a weapon is readily available (eg, in a holster or scabbard, slung over the back, et) drawing the weapon takes one combat round IF itis not immediately available, drawing it may take more than one round, at the gamemasters discretion.
